text: Recently, a cerium-based metal–organic framework (Ce-MOF) named as Ce-4,4′,4′′-N3TIB has been prepared using hydrothermal strategy employing 4,4′,4′′-nitrilotribenzoic acid as the organic linkers. The highly Lewis acidic sites of metal node Ce3+and the triphenylamine part of the organic linker rendered it as a highly stable Fenton-like heterogeneous catalyst. Its applications in heterogeneously catalytic oxidation of olefins, primary as well as secondary alcohols have also been studied. Remarkable yields of the targeted products have been obtained under optimized conditions using as prepared sample. The catalytic efficiency towards the oxidation of cinnamyl alcohol and styrene are 100% and 53%, respectively. The corresponding target products viz., benzaldehyde (100%) and styrene oxide (75%) are formed with very high selectivity and recovered catalyst exhibited good reusability up to 5-cycles. This as-prepared sample of Ce-MOF was also studied for the heterogeneous Advanced Oxidation Process (AOP) remediation of organic dyes such as Rhodamine B (RhB), Methyl Blue (MeB), Congo Red (CR), and Direct Blue (DB) using H2O2as an oxidant. •OH was considered as the major acting oxidizing species in heterogeneous catalytic reaction which thus supports the effectiveness of the obtained Ce-MOF as that of AOP catalysts.
explanation: The text shows domain-specific claims with minor typographical inconsistencies (e.g., 'Ce3+and', 'H2O2as') and slightly generic phrasing, which can indicate AI generation, though the technical content could also be from a real paper.
